description: A stylized screenshot of a Truth Social / X post by 'Donald J. Trump @realDonaldTrump,' rendered as a clean white card with black sans-serif body text. The avatar is Trump's face with an American-flag overlay; a magenta verified checkmark and black '+' subscription badge appear beside the name. Verbatim text: 'CNBC incorrectly reported that the Great Jensen Huang, of Nvidia, was not invited to the incredible gathering of the World's Greatest Businessmen/women proudly going to China. In actuality, Jensen is currently on Air Force One and, unless I ask him to leave, which is highly unlikely, CNBC's reporting is incorrect or, as they say in politics, FAKE NEWS! It is an Honor to have Jensen, Elon, Tim Apple, Larry Fink, Stephen Schwarzmann, Kelly Ortberg (Boeing), Brian Sikes (Cargill), Jane Fraser (Citi), Larry Culp (GE Aerospace), David Solomon (Goldman Sachs), Sanjay Mehrotra (Micron), Cristiano Amon (Qualcomm), and many others journeying to the Great Country of China where I will be asking President Xi, a Leader of extraordinary distinction, to "open up" China so that these brilliant people can work their magic, and help bring the People's Republic to an even higher level! In fact, I promise, that when we are together, which will be in a matter of hours, I will make that my very first request. I have never seen or heard of any idea that would be more beneficial to our incredible Countries! President DONALD J. TRUMP'. Messaging mixes name-dropping of top CEOs to project economic clout with an attack on CNBC as 'Fake News.' Propaganda technique: association with elite business figures, denigration of the press, and self-promotion. Target audience is the administration's base and business/financial followers.
notable_text: CNBC incorrectly reported that the Great Jensen Huang, of Nvidia, was not invited... as they say in politics, FAKE NEWS! ... journeying to the Great Country of China where I will be asking President Xi... to "open up" China.
